title: stachys anisochila subsp. zepcensis
intro: Stachys anisochila subsp. zepcensis, also known as the Serbian hedging mint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Traditionally, it is prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to be used as an herbal remedy for coughs, colds, and sore throats.
